# Orchestrator lms lane restart-loops ‚Äî needs HTTP-probe liveness

## Context

With `orchestrator.lms.enabled=true` (operator-enabled 2026-05-31, after the unified-Chroma migration), the orchestrator's `lms server (LM Studio CLI)` lane spins in a restart-loop. Operator flagged it ("this does not look right") with the spec: *"like for chroma, there has to be a check. running ‚áí all good, no logs. not running ‚áí start."*

Empirically reproduced in the operator's orchestrator log (PID 1666, four cycles 16:56:03‚Äì16:56:50): each cycle logs `Starting lms server (supervisor-restart)` ‚Üí stderr `Success! Server is now running on port 1234` ‚Üí `completed successfully`, then re-spawns ~15s later.

## The Problem

**#11986** (CLOSED 2026-05-25) added the lms lane to the orchestrator, mirroring the `mlx` task. Critically, #11986's body specified the correct idempotency primitive:

> *"Idempotency primitive: the existing `InferenceLifecycleService.isInferenceRunning()` HTTP probe to `/v1/models` is the canonical collision-defense check. New orchestrator task should reuse the same probe shape rather than re-implementing."*

The shipped implementation did **not** wire that HTTP probe into the supervision restart-decision. Instead the lms lane is supervised by **process-liveness** (`expectedCommand: 'lms server'` match, like `chroma`/`mlx`). But `lms server start` is **fire-and-exit**: it wakes LM Studio's background service (~5s) and exits 0. LM Studio's actual long-lived server process is **not** named `lms server`, so the supervisor's `expectedCommand` match never finds the lane "running" ‚Üí it re-spawns `lms server start` every poll/backoff cycle (~15s).

Non-fatal (LM Studio stays up on :1234; embedding works; re-runs are idempotent) but it produces continuous log noise (incl. the success banner on stderr logged at ERROR) and re-fires the `postSpawn` model-readiness check every cycle.

Contrast: `chroma` doesn't loop because the chroma daemon stays **foreground** (its process matches `expectedCommand: 'chroma'`); `mlx`'s `mlx_lm.server` likewise stays foreground. The lms launcher is the lone fire-and-exit case ‚Äî the exact distinction #11986's mirror-of-`mlx` missed.

## The Architectural Reality

- `ai/daemons/orchestrator/TaskDefinitions.mjs` (lms task, ~158): `command: 'lms'`, `args: ['server','start','--port',lmsPort]`, `expectedCommand: 'lms server'`, `pidFileName: 'lms.pid'`, `postSpawn: ensureLmsModelsLoaded(...)`.
- `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/ProcessSupervisorService.mjs`: the restart-decision uses `command.includes(task.expectedCommand)` process-match for liveness (~143, ~452) + restart-when-not-running. The LM-Studio nuance is *partially* noted (~257: "the HTTP server can listen while no chat/embedding model is resident" ‚Üí hence the `postSpawn` readiness hook) ‚Äî but the launcher's fire-and-exit shape is not handled for liveness.
- `ai/services/memory-core/lifecycle/InferenceLifecycleService.mjs`: `isInferenceRunning()` ‚Äî the HTTP probe to `/v1/models` that #11986 cited as canonical.
- `ai/services/graph/ProviderReadinessHelper.mjs`: `ensureLmsModelsLoaded` already HTTP-probes the OpenAI-compatible endpoint (host:port) ‚Äî reusable readiness primitive.
- Liveness target: `AiConfig.openAiCompatible.host` + `AiConfig.orchestrator.lms.port` (:1234), `GET /v1/models`.

## The Fix

Gate the lms lane's supervision restart-decision on an **HTTP liveness probe** of the OpenAI-compatible endpoint (host:port `GET /v1/models`, via `isInferenceRunning()` / `ProviderReadinessHelper`), **not** the `expectedCommand` process-match ‚Äî i.e., wire in the idempotency primitive #11986 already specified.

- **Server up (HTTP probe OK) ‚Üí no-op, NO logs** (quiet steady-state ‚Äî operator's explicit requirement).
- **Server down (probe fails) ‚Üí `lms server start` + `ensureLmsModelsLoaded`** (start + pre-warm).
- The exiting launcher's PID/`expectedCommand` is **not** the liveness signal for this lane; the HTTP endpoint is.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The lms lane's liveness/restart-decision uses an HTTP probe of the configured OpenAI-compatible host:port (`GET /v1/models`), not the `lms server` process-match.
- [ ] Server up ‚Üí orchestrator does NOT re-run `lms server start` and emits NO per-cycle logs (quiet steady-state; the ~15s loop is gone in a live orchestrator run with LM Studio up).
- [ ] Server down ‚Üí orchestrator runs `lms server start` + `ensureLmsModelsLoaded` to (re)start + pre-warm the embedding model.
- [ ] `postSpawn` model-readiness fires only on an actual (re)start, not every cycle.
- [ ] `mlx` lane audited for the same fire-and-exit class ‚Äî fixed if it shares it, or documented-as-unaffected (`mlx_lm.server` foreground) inline.
- [ ] Reuses the existing HTTP-probe primitive (`isInferenceRunning` / `ProviderReadinessHelper`) rather than re-implementing.

## Out of Scope

- The embedding provider/model choice (`openAiCompatible` + `qwen3-embedding-8b` stays).
- The `chroma`/`mlx` lanes except for the mlx same-class audit above.
- `orchestrator.lms.enabled` config semantics (already correct; this is the supervision shape).

## Avoided Traps

- **Reopening #11986** ‚Äî it's CLOSED/shipped (it added the lane); this is a distinct implementation defect, filed fresh citing it.
- **Re-introducing a retired `autoStartInference` per-MCP-server flag** (see `ai/scripts/diagnostics/check-retired-primitives.mjs` `RETIRED_CONFIG_FLAGS`) ‚Äî the orchestrator owns inference lifecycle via this lane; the fix is the lane's liveness model, not a new flag.
- **Disabling the lane** (loses orchestrator-managed LM Studio) ‚Äî rejected; the operator wants the orchestrator to own it.

## Decision Record impact

`none` ‚Äî orchestrator supervision bug-fix; no ADR change.

## Related

- **#11986** (origin of the lms lane; CLOSED ‚Äî this fixes the supervision-liveness gap in its implementation)
- **#11093 / PR #11096** (centralized daemon supervision ‚Äî the regression lineage)
- **#9833** (original LM Studio auto-boot + the `isInferenceRunning` `/v1/models` probe)
- **#12072** (Provider-readiness substrate / `ProviderReadinessHelper`)

- 2026-05-31T17:45:58Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `7f8750f` - "refactor(orchestrator): move liveness-gated restart into ProcessSupervisorService (#12262)

Per review feedback, the orchestrator poll loop must stay a thin scheduler with no task-supervision detail. Move the liveness-gated (re)start decision out of Orchestrator.poll into ProcessSupervisorService.superviseTask / gateRestartOnLivenessProbe ‚Äî the supervision owner, whose design note already mandates a provider-agnostic supervisor with task-owned readiness contracts. The poll loop now delegates in one line. Strip the ticket-id anchors from the .mjs comments (durable-artifact anti-pattern). Add direct superviseTask unit tests at the supervisor layer; the orchestrator spec now exercises the delegation.

Co-Authored-By: neo-opus-ada <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-31T17:56:28Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `e71ddea` - "test(orchestrator): align stale lms-default assertion with enabled-by-default config (#12262)

GPT cycle-1 RA1: the daemon.spec.mjs assertion (AiConfig.orchestrator.lms ships canonical LM Studio launch defaults) still expected leaf(false) for lms.enabled, but ba5cdee5d flipped the default to leaf(true) ‚Äî failing the unit CI job on this PR head. Updated the assertion to leaf(true); the model id and port are unchanged. Operator confirmed the red CI traces to that config change. The enable-default policy itself (default-on lms + aggressive chat preload) is tracked as a separate regression follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: neo-opus-ada <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>"

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-05-31T18:00:38Z

**L4 residual annotation** (per PR #12263 cycle-1 review RA2, @neo-gpt)

PR #12263 delivers the code fix (HTTP-probe liveness; L2 unit-verified ‚Äî 376 orchestrator unit tests green). The following live behaviors are **`[L4-deferred ‚Äî operator handoff needed]`** ‚Äî they require a live orchestrator + LM Studio run, which CI cannot perform:

- **AC2** ‚Äî server up ‚áí NO per-cycle logs; the ~15s `supervisor-restart` loop is gone in a live run.
- **AC3 (live path)** ‚Äî server down ‚áí exactly one `lms server start` + pre-warm (recovery behavior).

`Resolves #12262` retains the auto-close on merge (the code is delivered); the live quiet-steady-state confirmation is tracked in PR #12263's **Post-Merge Validation** checklist as the operator handoff. If live validation surfaces residual loop behavior, re-open.

Out of scope here and tracked separately: the orthogonal lms **preload-safety regression** (default-on lane force-loading an oversized chat model ‚Äî `gemma-4-31b-it @ 262K`) ‚Üí **#12264**.

- 2026-05-31T18:06:49Z @tobiu referenced in commit `2b48f2d` - "fix(orchestrator): HTTP-probe liveness for fire-and-exit lms lane (#12262) (#12263)

* fix(orchestrator): HTTP-probe liveness for fire-and-exit lms lane (#12262)

lms server start is fire-and-exit (wakes the LM Studio service, then returns), so the served process never matches expectedCommand "lms server" for the supervisor process-match check ‚Äî !state.running stays permanently true and the lane re-spawns every cooldown, flooding the logs. Mirror the chroma check-then-start shape: gate the restart on an HTTP liveness probe of the OpenAI-compatible endpoint (GET /v1/models via ProviderReadinessHelper). Healthy => silent no-op (re-probed one cooldown out; confirmed-at + in-flight guards prevent a per-poll probe/log storm); down => lms server start + ensureLmsModelsLoaded postSpawn. mlx lane audited: python -m mlx_lm.server is a genuine foreground process matching expectedCommand, so it is not in this fire-and-exit class (unchanged).

Co-Authored-By: neo-opus-ada <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>

* refactor(orchestrator): move liveness-gated restart into ProcessSupervisorService (#12262)

Per review feedback, the orchestrator poll loop must stay a thin scheduler with no task-supervision detail. Move the liveness-gated (re)start decision out of Orchestrator.poll into ProcessSupervisorService.superviseTask / gateRestartOnLivenessProbe ‚Äî the supervision owner, whose design note already mandates a provider-agnostic supervisor with task-owned readiness contracts. The poll loop now delegates in one line. Strip the ticket-id anchors from the .mjs comments (durable-artifact anti-pattern). Add direct superviseTask unit tests at the supervisor layer; the orchestrator spec now exercises the delegation.

Co-Authored-By: neo-opus-ada <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>

* test(orchestrator): align stale lms-default assertion with enabled-by-default config (#12262)

GPT cycle-1 RA1: the daemon.spec.mjs assertion (AiConfig.orchestrator.lms ships canonical LM Studio launch defaults) still expected leaf(false) for lms.enabled, but ba5cdee5d flipped the default to leaf(true) ‚Äî failing the unit CI job on this PR head. Updated the assertion to leaf(true); the model id and port are unchanged. Operator confirmed the red CI traces to that config change. The enable-default policy itself (default-on lms + aggressive chat preload) is tracked as a separate regression follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: neo-opus-ada <neo-opus-ada@neomjs.com>
